body.title: [sprint] Consequence check: 4-tier memory scope + global KB
body.description:
**What changed:** 4-tier memory scope (global/local/agent/client) with separate DBs; global tier is a forkable git repo.

**Predicted unintended effects:**
- DB connection count explosion — each device opens connections to 3+ DBs; connection pool limits hit at scale
- Global KB diverges across forks without upstream PRs — the commons fragments
- Client data isolation breaks if a device caches a cross-client query result in local/shared scope
- 'Agent requests other agent's data via bus' protocol is never implemented — agents just query the DB directly because it's easier
- Migration from Igor-wild-0001 single DB is deferred indefinitely — new tier model ships but old data stays in the monolith

**Signals to watch:**
- Postgres connection count per device (alert if >20)
- Global KB fork divergence (check upstream PRs monthly)
- Any direct cross-DB query outside bus protocol (grep for connection strings in device code)
- Migration ticket status (should have a filed execution ticket within 30 days of T-memory-scope-layers closing)

**Gate condition:** Check by 2026-06-15.

**Affected files:** None — observation only
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** Observe; annotate as occurred / clear / partial and close
**Completion criteria:** Observation ticket — done when each predicted effect is annotated
